channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Rare Blue Micromoon to rise this weekend - MLive.com
item.description:
1. Rare Blue Micromoon to rise this weekend  MLive.com
  2. How and when to watch May’s blue moon  CNN
  3. How to Best See This Weekend’s Rare Blue Micromoon  Time Magazine
  4. Rare Blue Moon hides the red light of an ancient star on May 31 — Will you be able to see it?  Space
  5. A rare blue micromoon arrives this weekend and won't be back until December 2028. Here's what to expect.  CBS News

item.title: NASA's next major space telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman, is expected to find roughly 100,000 new transiting planets in just five years — along with the largest catalogue ever assembled of rogue worlds drifting through the galaxy without a star to orbit - Space Daily
item.description:
1. NASA's next major space telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman, is expected to find roughly 100,000 new transiting planets in just five years — along with the largest catalogue ever assembled of rogue worlds drifting through the galaxy without a star to orbit  Space Daily
  2. Peering into the Milky Way's far side, Roman could unveil 100,000 worlds  Phys.org
  3. Roman telescope will observe an unexplored part of the Milky Way  Universe Space Tech
  4. Moment of Science: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope set to revolutionize astronomy  WTVG
  5. NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look  NASA (.gov)

item.title: Mars's manganese 'bathtub ring' reveals ancient ocean timeline and its potential for life - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Mars's manganese 'bathtub ring' reveals ancient ocean timeline and its potential for life  Phys.org

  3. Carbon-Rich Rocks May Have Cooled the Ancient Martian Atmosphere  eos.org
  4. Mineral ring on Mars reveals an ocean that lasted a million years  Earth.com
  5. Mineral clues in Gale Crater track ancient Mars climate change  Phys.org

item.title: To travel far on cloudy days, pigeons listen to their gut - CNN
item.description:
1. To travel far on cloudy days, pigeons listen to their gut  CNN
  2. How do pigeons sense Earth's magnetic field? Scientists say they’ve solved the mystery.  National Geographic
  3. Pigeons may be navigating with their liver, study suggests  AP News
  4. Pigeons navigate using magnetic sensors in livers — study  DW.com
  5. A new study says homing pigeon livers act like compasses. Other experts aren’t so sure  Scientific American

item.title: Companies like SpaceX want electromagnetic catapults on the moon. Could they be used as weapons? - Space
item.description:
1. Companies like SpaceX want electromagnetic catapults on the moon. Could they be used as weapons?  Space

  4. NASA is testing a cancer drug in space right now  Yahoo
  5. Could space-grown stem cells unlock faster cancer treatments and revolutionize organ regeneration breakthr  The Economic Times

item.title: Scientists Found a Black Hole That Breaks The Rules of Astrophysics - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Scientists Found a Black Hole That Breaks The Rules of Astrophysics  ScienceAlert
  2. A direct black-hole mass measurement in a little red dot at high redshift  Nature
  3. Little Red Dot Abell2744-QSO1: Sonification of Gas Velocity Around a Supermassive Black Hole (NIRCam and NIRSpec IFU)  NASA Science (.gov)
  4. James Webb Space Telescope discovers a black hole that formed before its host galaxy. Scientists aren't sure how  Space
  5. 'A Paradigm Shift': Supermassive Black Hole Without a Galaxy Changes What We Thought Came First  Gizmodo

item.title: Severed sea cucumber appendages don’t seem to die - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. Severed sea cucumber appendages don’t seem to die  Ars Technica
  2. A severed piece of sea cucumber refused to die, and what happened next could transform medicine  Phys.org
  3. Can Cells from a Sea Cucumber Live Forever?  Nautilus | Science
  4. Sea cucumber tissue heals itself and survives for years after being detached from the body  Earth.com
  5. A Marine Animal Defies the Limits of Immortality: A Fragment of Its Amputated Body Survives for Three Years in Seawater  La Brújula Verde

item.title: Here's how Tyrannosaurus ended up with a big head and small arms - Reuters
item.description:
1. Here's how Tyrannosaurus ended up with a big head and small arms  Reuters
  2. The mystery of T. rex’s tiny arms might finally be solved  CNN
  3. Tyrannosaurus Rex and Other Terrifying Predatory Dinosaurs Had Itty-Bitty Arms. Scientists May Have Finally Figured Out Why  Smithsonian Magazine
  4. T. rex had massive jaws but tiny arms: Scientists now know why  The Independent
  5. Why Do T-Rex Dinosaurs Have Mini Arms? Scientists May Have Discovered the Reason  Yahoo

item.title: Bizarre beaked reptile from New Mexico turns out to be a toothless cousin of crocodiles - Yahoo
item.description:

  4. These Ancient Crocodiles Walked On Two Legs And Had Beaks!  DOGO News
  5. Toothless, Bipedal Crocodile Relative Lived in New Mexico 212 Million Years Ago  Sci.News

item.title: The surface of the Sun is around 5,500 degrees, but its outer atmosphere is more than a million degrees hotter, and after decades of work physicists still cannot fully explain how the cooler layer heats the one above it. - Space Daily
item.description: The surface of the Sun is around 5,500 degrees, but its outer atmosphere is more than a million degrees hotter, and after decades of work physicists still cannot fully explain how the cooler layer heats the one above it.  Space Daily

item.title: The asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs may have struck a planet that was already in serious trouble — a new Johns Hopkins study has found evidence of a separate ecological crisis that began about 30,000 years before the impact, coincident with a high-vo - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs may have struck a planet that was already in serious trouble — a new Johns Hopkins study has found evidence of a separate ecological crisis that began about 30,000 years before the impact, coincident with a high-vo  Space Daily
  2. Fungi Bloomed Twice around End-Cretaceous Mass Extinction  Sci.News
  3. When The Dinosaur-Killing Asteroid Hit, This Life-Form Feasted On The Death  ScienceAlert
  4. Extinction: What were conditions like on Earth when the dinosaur-killing asteroid crashed into the planet?  FOX Weather
  5. Story of dinosaur-killer meteorite impact told in vivid detail  The Guardian

item.title: Astronomers have found a cloud of water vapour around a distant quasar holding something like 140 trillion times all the water in Earth's oceans, the largest reservoir of water ever discovered anywhere in the universe. - Space Daily
item.description: Astronomers have found a cloud of water vapour around a distant quasar holding something like 140 trillion times all the water in Earth's oceans, the largest reservoir of water ever discovered anywhere in the universe.  Space Daily

item.title: The 'Doomsday Glacier' is poised to lose its ice shelf this year. An Antarctic researcher explains what that means for global sea levels - Live Science
item.description:
1. The 'Doomsday Glacier' is poised to lose its ice shelf this year. An Antarctic researcher explains what that means for global sea levels  Live Science
  2. A torpedo-sized submarine robot was launched through a 600-meter hole in the "doomsday glacier" and revealed cracks melting up to 10 times faster; Icefin showed from the inside how warm water threatens Thwaites, an ice mass capable of raising sea levels  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  3. Scientists Warn Antarctica's 'Doomsday Glacier' Could Collapse — But Is There a Bigger Picture?  Charisma Magazine Online
  4. Ice shelf the size of Indian capital city Delhi will detach from 'Doomsday Glacier' in 2026  WION
  5. Ice shelf the size of Delhi to detach from Doomsday Glacier this year | Thwaites is largest glacier in West Antarctica | Inshorts  Inshorts

item.title: A pair of American satellites built to catch the Soviets cheating on a nuclear test ban kept detecting unexplained flashes, and the flashes turned out to be the most powerful explosions in the universe coming from billions of light-years away - Space Daily
item.description: A pair of American satellites built to catch the Soviets cheating on a nuclear test ban kept detecting unexplained flashes, and the flashes turned out to be the most powerful explosions in the universe coming from billions of light-years away  Space Daily

item.title: In 1987 a star exploded in a nearby galaxy, and detectors deep underground caught the burst of neutrinos roughly three hours before any telescope on Earth saw the light arrive. - Space Daily
item.description: In 1987 a star exploded in a nearby galaxy, and detectors deep underground caught the burst of neutrinos roughly three hours before any telescope on Earth saw the light arrive.  Space Daily
